name: William Randolph Hearst
abstract: William Randolph Hearst (; April 29, 1863 – August 14, 1951) was an American newspaper publisher and politician. He laid the foundation for the Hearst family's mass media empire via his control of the Hearst Corporation.

concerts.label: Meeting: Protest Against Russian Passport Discrimination

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: Interviewer mentions Hearst came to the Alabam; Interviewee does not respond with personal connection or confirmation of meeting him.

about_quotes.speaker: Lawrence Brown
about_quotes.evidence: [INTERVIEWER] Edgar Mobley (PHONETIC) told me that they had all the movie stars of Hollywood, William Randolph Hearst -- [INTERVIEWEE] Everybody.

about_quotes.speaker: Patricia Willard
about_quotes.evidence: William Randolph Hearst never came on the Avenue in his life because he was from some sort of affliction

about_quotes.speaker: Annie Ross
about_quotes.evidence: Marion Davies, who was William Randolph Hearst’s love, left her all her money. So Gladys was rich.
about_quotes.doc_title: Annie Ross — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Andrew Blakeney
about_quotes.evidence: He was a very conservative gentleman, as was the whole family.
